description: Informal note without the names of the sender or addressee (as was common in notes exchanged between friends). In Judaeo-Arabic. Written on a long vertical strip of paper. Goitein suggests that the scribe is Shelomo b. Eliyyahu, but this does not seem right. The hand does appear in other documents from Shelomo's period including ENA NS 75.20 (PGPID 12255). Dating: Probably late 12th or 13th century. The sender opens, "What is the reason for this anger?! It is senseless and misplaced." He apologizes for not meeting the addressee out of fear that they would attract a crowd at the synagogue. He wants the addressee to come see him: "from the day that you departed... my temperament has not been balanced." (Information in part from Goitein's index card and Med Soc V, p. 505n7.)
